title: SecureEnvelope KAT
description: Known-Answer Test vectors for the auths pairing `SecureEnvelope` AEAD wrapping. The canonical file lives in this repo at `crates/auths-pairing-protocol/tests/vectors/secure_envelope.json`; mobile and other cross-implementation mirrors reference this schema via `$schema` and must validate against it.

envelope_info.description: HKDF `info` string / domain separator. Must equal `auths-pairing-envelope-v1` for version 1 of the envelope; mirrors MUST reject vectors that declare any other value.

aead.description: AEAD primitive. ChaCha20-Poly1305 in the default build; AES-256-GCM under FIPS/CNSA builds (separate KAT file).

version.description: Schema/format version. Bump on any wire-format change.

vectors.description: Ordered list of KAT vectors. Order is significant: drift in ordering is treated as a file mutation by the generator.

name.description: Human-readable identifier for the vector. Stable across regenerations.

transport_key_hex.description: Input: 32-byte transport key, hex-encoded (64 hex chars).

iv_hex.description: Input: 12-byte per-session IV, hex-encoded (24 hex chars).

session_id.description: Input: pairing session id (part of AAD).

counter.description: Input: monotonic counter for this envelope. Valid range is [1, 1024).

plaintext_utf8.description: Input: plaintext as UTF-8 string. Mutually exclusive with `plaintext_hex`; exactly one must be present across both fields (modulo the empty-string case where `plaintext_utf8` is an empty string).

plaintext_hex.description: Input: plaintext as hex-encoded bytes. Used for binary or oversize vectors where UTF-8 rendering is unhelpful. Mutually exclusive with `plaintext_utf8`.

nonce_hex.description: Output: expected 12-byte AEAD nonce (`iv XOR counter_be` on the last 4 bytes).

aad_hex.description: Output: expected AAD — `u32_be(len(session_id)) || session_id || u32_be(len(path)) || path || u32_be(counter)`.

ciphertext_hex.description: Output: expected ChaCha20-Poly1305 ciphertext with 16-byte Poly1305 tag appended.

expected_result.description: For positive vectors: `"valid"` — the opener must recover the exact plaintext. For negative vectors: the declared `EnvelopeError` kind (kebab-case).

mutation.description: Negative vectors only: short natural-language description of the mutation applied. Ignored for positive vectors.
